design_philosophy.core_loop: Select club, aim trajectory, execute swing with power/precision meter, putt on green, complete hole, advance through courses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing power control against precision to land ball on fairway and green; putting accuracy under pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Authentic golf simulation—experiencing the feel and challenge of professional golf without leaving home
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strong commitment to realism and authentic golf mechanics (realistic putting feel emphasized by reviewers); accessibility to non-golfers while maintaining depth for enthusiasts; course design philosophy that includes water hazards as primary obstacles but evolves to include trees, bunkers, and varied landscapes across iterations

difficulty.checkpointing: Per-hole progression; courses are completable in single sessions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple courses of varying difficulty; player can choose which course to attempt; practice modes available in later iterations (World Class featured overhead course overview for strategy planning)

progression.unlock_mechanism: Multiple courses available from start; expansion disks provided additional courses (Leaderboard Tournament expansion format); World Class included four courses: Cypress Creek, Doral Country Club, St Andrews, and fictional Gauntlet Country Club with three additional expansion disks
replayability_hooks: Multiple distinct courses with different hazard layouts
Course editor feature in World Class allowing custom modifications
Real golf courses (St Andrews) alongside fictional designs
Expansion disk model encouraging iterative replay

world_building_techniques: Real-world course locations (St Andrews) grounding simulation in authentic golf heritage
Fictional course (Gauntlet Country Club) providing fantasy challenge variant
Visual representation of course geography and hazards as environmental storytelling

environmental_storytelling: Water hazard placement and layout defines course identity and difficulty
Progressive course evolution (Executive Edition added trees and bunkers; World Class added varied landscape features) demonstrates technical/artistic development
Mix of authentic courses and fictional designs creates strategic variety

sources_used: wikipedia: Leader Board series article covering design features, release history, and critical reception across all iterations
